reasoning: The document exhibits very high content fidelity (composite score 0.997) but contains two programmatic major defects: a full-page image alongside extracted text and mixed numbering types in lists indicating possible sub-point flattening. According to the count-based routing rules, the presence of 1-5 major defects requires a routing to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (2 list(s)): p1:letter+roman, p5:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=2. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The source document uses bold formatting for the beginning words of each list item in the comment summaries (e.g., 'Environmental:', 'Property Values:', 'Health Issues:'). This bold emphasis is completely lost in the JSON output, as no markdown syntax is present.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mi
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Check for and remove redundant full-page images if the content is already extracted as text
Review list structures on pages 1 and 5 to ensure proper nesting and sub-point hierarchy
Remove any remaining header/footer artifacts from the body text
Restore bold emphasis to the comment summaries if required
